name: Ted Rosenthal
abstract: Ted Rosenthal (born 1959) is an American jazz pianist. He was featured on David Sanborn's series Night Music, and has performed worldwide, both as a leader and as a sideman with many jazz greats, including Gerry Mulligan, Art Farmer, Phil Woods, Bob Brookmeyer, and Jon Faddis.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: Also my second recording I made with a wonderful jazz pianist, Ted Rosenthal... And he was on my second record and I’ve had occasion to play with Ted a few other times
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Adrian Cunningham
about_quotes.evidence: I just wanted to write stuff that I could hear them sounding great on and hopefully they didn't complain too much so I think it was pretty good. ... Jeff, John and Ted Rosenthal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Adrian Cunningham interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 31, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Rosenthal was in school, a guy named Roy Gerson, Manny Mendelson, other names will come back to me — some really, really fine players were in school at that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.evidence: No direct statement from Dave Brubeck regarding Ted Rosenthal; the mention is made by the Interviewer.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: I did it with Jay and a piano player by the name of Ted Rosenthal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023
